revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「{{神社 |名称= 轟神社 |画像= |所在地= [[徳島県]][[海部郡 (徳島県)|海部郡]][[海陽町]]平井字王余魚谷21-1 |位置= |祭神=…」
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 轟神社 |画像= |所在地= [[徳島県]][[海部郡 (徳島県)|海部郡]][[海陽町]]平井字王余魚谷21-1 |位置= |祭神= [[ミヅハノメ|水象女命]]
[[国狭槌尊]]
[[大山祇命]] |社格= |創建= [[天正]]年間（[[1573年]]-[[1593年]]） |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |}} '''轟神社'''（とどろきじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[海陽町]]にある[[神社]]である。 == 歴史 == [[天正]]年間（[[1573年]]-[[1593年]]）に社殿を建立。周囲にある[[轟九十九滝]]を神霊の坐すところとして現在に至るまで信仰されている。また[[徳島藩]]の藩祖である[[蜂須賀家政]]が、[[朝鮮出兵]]にあたり海上安全を祈願したとも伝えられている{{cite web|url=https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html|title=轟神社|publisher=阿波ナビ|accessdate=2021-4-23}}。 毎年秋には轟神社および轟九十九滝で例大祭が行われ、白装束に身を包んだ男達が轟神社から御輿を担ぎ140段の急な石段を駆け下りる{{cite web|url=https://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki|title=轟秋祭り|publisher=海陽町観光協会|accessdate=2021-4-23}}。 == 祭神 == * [[ミヅハノメ|水象女命]] * [[国狭槌尊]] * [[大山祇命]] == 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[牟岐線]]「[[阿波海南駅]]」下車より町営バス「轟神社」下車、徒歩で約10分。 == 脚注 == == 外部リンク == * [https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html 轟神社] - 阿波ナビ {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ととろきしんしや}} [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:海陽町]]

revision.comment: 改名提案テンプレート貼付
revision.text:
{{改名提案|轟神社_(徳島県海陽町)|date=2021年4月}} {{神社 |名称= 轟神社 |画像= |所在地= [[徳島県]][[海部郡 (徳島県)|海部郡]][[海陽町]]平井字王余魚谷21-1 |位置= |祭神= [[ミヅハノメ|水象女命]]
[[国狭槌尊]]
[[大山祇命]] |社格= |創建= [[天正]]年間（[[1573年]]-[[1593年]]） |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |}} '''轟神社'''（とどろきじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[海陽町]]にある[[神社]]である。 == 歴史 == [[天正]]年間（[[1573年]]-[[1593年]]）に社殿を建立。周囲にある[[轟九十九滝]]を神霊の坐すところとして現在に至るまで信仰されている。また[[徳島藩]]の藩祖である[[蜂須賀家政]]が、[[朝鮮出兵]]にあたり海上安全を祈願したとも伝えられている{{cite web|url=https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html|title=轟神社|publisher=阿波ナビ|accessdate=2021-4-23}}。 毎年秋には轟神社および轟九十九滝で例大祭が行われ、白装束に身を包んだ男達が轟神社から御輿を担ぎ140段の急な石段を駆け下りる{{cite web|url=https://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki|title=轟秋祭り|publisher=海陽町観光協会|accessdate=2021-4-23}}。 == 祭神 == * [[ミヅハノメ|水象女命]] * [[国狭槌尊]] * [[大山祇命]] == 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[牟岐線]]「[[阿波海南駅]]」下車より町営バス「轟神社」下車、徒歩で約10分。 == 脚注 == == 外部リンク == * [https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html 轟神社] - 阿波ナビ {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ととろきしんしや}} [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:海陽町]]

{{神社 |名称= 轟神社 |画像= {{画像募集中|cat=徳島県海部郡}} |所在地= [[徳島県]][[海部郡 (徳島県)|海部郡]][[海陽町]]平井字王余魚谷21-1 |位置= |祭神= [[ミヅハノメ|水象女命]]
[[国狭槌尊]]
[[大山祇命]] |社格= |創建= [[天正]]年間（[[1573年]]-[[1593年]]） |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= ||緯度度=33|緯度分=34|緯度秒=54.25|経度度=134|経度分=15|経度秒=16.00}} '''轟神社'''（とどろきじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[海陽町]]にある[[神社]]である。 == 歴史 == [[天正]]年間（[[1573年]]-[[1593年]]）に社殿を建立。周囲にある[[轟九十九滝]]を神霊の坐すところとして現在に至るまで信仰されている。また[[徳島藩]]の藩祖である[[蜂須賀家政]]が、[[文禄・慶長の役|朝鮮出兵]]にあたり海上安全を祈願したとも伝えられている{{cite web|url=https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html|title=轟神社|publisher=阿波ナビ|accessdate=2021-4-23}}。 毎年秋には轟神社および轟九十九滝で例大祭が行われ、白装束に身を包んだ男達が轟神社から御輿を担ぎ140段の急な石段を駆け下りる{{cite web|url=https://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki|title=轟秋祭り|publisher=海陽町観光協会|accessdate=2021-4-23}}。 == 祭神 == * [[ミヅハノメ|水象女命]] * [[国狭槌尊]] * [[大山祇命]] == 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[牟岐線]]「[[阿波海南駅]]」下車より町営バス「轟神社」下車、徒歩で約10分。 == 脚注 == == 外部リンク == * [https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html 轟神社] - 阿波ナビ {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ととろきしんしや}} [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:海陽町の建築物]] [[Category:海陽町の歴史]]

'''Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)'''
[[ja:%E8%BD%9F%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E6%B5%B7%E9%99%BD%E7%94%BA)]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]]

{{Shrine | Name = Todoroki Shrine | Image = {{Image requested|cat=Kaifu District, Tokushima}} | Location = {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Kaifu District, Tokushima|ja|海部郡 (徳島県)}} {{ill|Kaiyō|ja|海陽町}} Hirai Ōyo-godani 21-1 | Coordinates = {{Coord|33|34|54.25|N|134|15|16.00|E|scale:5000_region:JP|display=inline}} | Deities = {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミヅハノメ}}
{{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊}}
{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命}} | Rank = | Founded = During the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正}} (1573–1593) | MainBuilding = | AlternateName = | AnnualFestival = | Ceremonies = }} '''Todoroki Shrine''' (轟神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kaiyō|ja|海陽町}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, Japan. == History == Todoroki Shrine was established during the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正}} (1573–1593). The surrounding {{ill|Todoroki 99 Falls|ja|轟九十九滝}} have been revered as a sacred site where divine spirits dwell. According to tradition, {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政}}, the founder of the {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, prayed at the shrine for safe sea voyages during the {{ill|Bunroku and Keichō Campaigns|ja|文禄・慶長の役}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html |title=Todoroki Shrine |publisher=Awa Navi |accessdate=2021-04-23}}. Each autumn, a grand festival is held at Todoroki Shrine and Todoroki 99 Falls. During the festival, men dressed in white robes carry a portable shrine ({{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}}) and rush down 140 steep stone steps from Todoroki Shrine, creating a dramatic and sacred spectacle{{Cite web|url=https://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |title=Todoroki Autumn Festival |publisher=Kaiyō Tourism Association |accessdate=2021-04-23 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130423021314/http://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp:80/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |archive-date=2013-04-23}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミヅハノメ}} (Goddess of Water) * {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊}} (Deity of the Land) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命}} (Mountain God) == Transportation == * Take the {{ill|JR Shikoku|ja|四国旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線}} to {{ill|Awa-Kainan Station|ja|阿波海南駅}}, then transfer to a town bus to the "Todoroki Shrine" stop. The shrine is a 10-minute walk from the stop. == References == == External Links == * [https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html Todoroki Shrine] - Awa Navi {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kaiyō]] [[Category:History of Kaiyō]] [[ja:%E8%BD%9F%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E6%B5%B7%E9%99%BD%E7%94%BA)]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]]

{{Shrine | Name = Todoroki Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=Kaifu District, Tokushima}} | location = {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Kaifu District, Tokushima|ja|海部郡 (徳島県)}} {{ill|Kaiyō|ja|海陽町}} Hirai Ōyo-godani 21-1 | Coordinates = {{Coord|33|34|54.25|N|134|15|16.00|E|scale:5000_region:JP|display=inline}} | Deities = {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミヅハノメ}}
{{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊}}
{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命}} | Rank = | Founded = During the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正}} (1573–1593) | MainBuilding = | AlternateName = | AnnualFestival = | Ceremonies = }} '''Todoroki Shrine''' (轟神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kaiyō|ja|海陽町}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, Japan. == History == Todoroki Shrine was established during the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正}} (1573–1593). According to tradition, {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政}}, the founder of the {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, prayed at the shrine for safe sea voyages during the {{ill|Bunroku and Keichō Campaigns|ja|文禄・慶長の役}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html |title=Todoroki Shrine |publisher=Awa Navi |accessdate=2021-04-23}}. The shrine is a 10-minute walk from the stop. == References == == External Links == * [https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html Todoroki Shrine] - Awa Navi {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kaiyō]] [[Category:History of Kaiyō]] [[ja:%E8%BD%9F%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E6%B5%B7%E9%99%BD%E7%94%BA)]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]]

Each autumn, a grand festival is held at Todoroki Shrine and Todoroki 99 Falls. The shrine is a 10-minute walk from the stop. == References == == External Links == * [https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html Todoroki Shrine] - Awa Navi {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kaiyō]] [[Category:History of Kaiyō]] [[ja:%E8%BD%9F%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E6%B5%B7%E9%99%BD%E7%94%BA)]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]]

During the festival, men dressed in white robes carry a portable shrine ({{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}}) and rush down 140 steep stone steps from Todoroki Shrine, creating a dramatic and sacred spectacle{{Cite web|url=https://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |title=Todoroki Autumn Festival |publisher=Kaiyō Tourism Association |accessdate=2021-04-23 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130423021314/http://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp:80/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |archive-date=2013-04-23}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミヅハノメ}} (Goddess of Water) * {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊}} (Deity of the Land) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命}} (Mountain God) == Transportation == * Take the {{ill|JR Shikoku|ja|四国旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線}} to {{ill|Awa-Kainan Station|ja|阿波海南駅}}, then transfer to a town bus to the "Todoroki Shrine" stop. The shrine is a 10-minute walk from the stop. == References == == External Links == * [https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html Todoroki Shrine] - Awa Navi {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kaiyō]] [[Category:History of Kaiyō]] [[ja:%E8%BD%9F%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E6%B5%B7%E9%99%BD%E7%94%BA)]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:kkkk]]

{{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊}}
{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命}} | Rank = | consecration_year = During the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正}} (1573–1593) | MainBuilding = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Ceremonies = }} '''Todoroki Shrine''' (轟神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kaiyō|ja|海陽町}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, Japan. == History == Todoroki Shrine was established during the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正}} (1573–1593). The surrounding {{ill|Todoroki 99 Falls|ja|轟九十九滝}} have been revered as a sacred site where divine spirits dwell. According to tradition, {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政}}, the founder of the {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, prayed at the shrine for safe sea voyages during the {{ill|Bunroku and Keichō Campaigns|ja|文禄・慶長の役}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html |title=Todoroki Shrine |publisher=Awa Navi |accessdate=2021-04-23}}. Each autumn, a grand festival is held at Todoroki Shrine and Todoroki 99 Falls. During the festival, men dressed in white robes carry a portable shrine ({{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}}) and rush down 140 steep stone steps from Todoroki Shrine, creating a dramatic and sacred spectacle{{Cite web|url=https://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |title=Todoroki Autumn Festival |publisher=Kaiyō Tourism Association |accessdate=2021-04-23 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130423021314/http://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp:80/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |archive-date=2013-04-23}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミヅハノメ}} (Goddess of Water) * {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊}} (deity of the Land) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命}} (Mountain God) == Transportation == * Take the {{ill|JR Shikoku|ja|四国旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線}} to {{ill|Awa-Kainan Station|ja|阿波海南駅}}, then transfer to a town bus to the "Todoroki Shrine" stop. The shrine is a 10-minute walk from the stop. == References == == External Links == * [https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html Todoroki Shrine] - Awa Navi {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kaiyō]] [[Category:History of Kaiyō]] [[ja:%E8%BD%9F%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E6%B5%B7%E9%99%BD%E7%94%BA)]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)-qq]] [[Category:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)-qq]] [[Category:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:qq]]

{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命}} | Rank = | consecration_year = During the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正}} (1573–1593) | MainBuilding = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Ceremonies = }} '''Todoroki Shrine''' (轟神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kaiyō|ja|海陽町}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, Japan. == History == Todoroki Shrine was established during the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正}} (1573–1593). The surrounding {{ill|Todoroki 99 Falls|ja|轟九十九滝}} have been revered as a sacred site where divine spirits dwell. According to tradition, {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政}}, the founder of the {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, prayed at the shrine for safe sea voyages during the {{ill|Bunroku and Keichō Campaigns|ja|文禄・慶長の役}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html |title=Todoroki Shrine |publisher=Awa Navi |accessdate=2021-04-23}}. Each autumn, a grand festival is held at Todoroki Shrine and Todoroki 99 Falls. During the festival, men dressed in white robes carry a portable shrine ({{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}}) and rush down 140 steep stone steps from Todoroki Shrine, creating a dramatic and sacred spectacle{{Cite web|url=https://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |title=Todoroki Autumn Festival |publisher=Kaiyō Tourism Association |accessdate=2021-04-23 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130423021314/http://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp:80/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |archive-date=2013-04-23}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミヅハノメ}} (Goddess of Water) * {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊}} (deity of the Land) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命}} (Mountain God) == Transportation == * Take the {{ill|JR Shikoku|ja|四国旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線}} to {{ill|Awa-Kainan Station|ja|阿波海南駅}}, then transfer to a town bus to the "Todoroki Shrine" stop. The shrine is a 10-minute walk from the stop. == References == == External Links == * [https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html Todoroki Shrine] - Awa Navi {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kaiyō]] [[Category:History of Kaiyō]] [[ja:%E8%BD%9F%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E6%B5%B7%E9%99%BD%E7%94%BA)]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)-qq]] [[Category:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)-qq]] [[Category:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]][[Category:qq]]

According to tradition, {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政}}, the founder of the {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, prayed at the shrine for safe sea voyages during the {{ill|Imjin War|lt=Bunroku and Keichō Campaigns|ja|文禄・慶長の役}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html |title=Todoroki Shrine |publisher=Awa Navi |accessdate=2021-04-23}}. Each autumn, a grand festival is held at Todoroki Shrine and Todoroki 99 Falls. During the festival, men dressed in white robes carry a portable shrine ({{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}}) and rush down 140 steep stone steps from Todoroki Shrine, creating a dramatic and sacred spectacle{{Cite web|url=https://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |title=Todoroki Autumn Festival |publisher=Kaiyō Tourism Association |accessdate=2021-04-23 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130423021314/http://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp:80/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |archive-date=2013-04-23}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミヅハノメ}} (Goddess of Water) * {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto}} (deity of the Land) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命}} (Mountain God) == Transportation == * Take the {{ill|JR Shikoku|ja|四国旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線}} to {{ill|Awa-Kainan Station|ja|阿波海南駅}}, then transfer to a town bus to the "Todoroki Shrine" stop. The shrine is a 10-minute walk from the stop. == References == == External Links == * [https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html Todoroki Shrine] - Awa Navi {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kaiyō]] [[Category:History of Kaiyō]] [[ja:%E8%BD%9F%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E6%B5%B7%E9%99%BD%E7%94%BA)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]

The surrounding {{ill|Todoroki 99 Falls|ja|轟九十九滝}} have been revered as a sacred site where divine spirits dwell. According to tradition, {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政}}, the founder of the {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, prayed at the shrine for safe sea voyages during the {{ill|Imjin War|lt=Bunroku and Keichō Campaigns|ja|文禄・慶長の役}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html |title=Todoroki Shrine |publisher=Awa Navi |accessdate=2021-04-23}}. Each autumn, a grand festival is held at Todoroki Shrine and Todoroki 99 Falls. During the festival, men dressed in white robes carry a portable shrine ({{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}}) and rush down 140 steep stone steps from Todoroki Shrine, creating a dramatic and sacred spectacle{{Cite web|url=https://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |title=Todoroki Autumn Festival |publisher=Kaiyō Tourism Association |accessdate=2021-04-23 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130423021314/http://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp:80/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |archive-date=2013-04-23}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミヅハノメ}} (Goddess of Water) * {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto}} (deity of the Land) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命}} (Mountain God) == Transportation == * Take the {{ill|JR Shikoku|ja|四国旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線}} to {{ill|Awa-Kainan Station|ja|阿波海南駅}}, then transfer to a town bus to the "Todoroki Shrine" stop. The shrine is a 10-minute walk from the stop. == References == == External Links == * [https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html Todoroki Shrine] - Awa Navi {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kaiyō]] [[Category:History of Kaiyō]] [[ja:%E8%BD%9F%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E6%B5%B7%E9%99%BD%E7%94%BA)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[category:qq close]] [[Category:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] [[category:qq close]] [[Category:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]][[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]][[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] [[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] [[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] [[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] [[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]][[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]][[Category:qq]]

According to tradition, {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政}}, the founder of the {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, prayed at the shrine for safe sea voyages during the {{ill|Imjin War|lt=Bunroku and Keichō Campaigns|ja|文禄・慶長の役}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html |title=Todoroki Shrine |publisher=Awa Navi |accessdate=2021-04-23}}. Each autumn, a grand festival is held at Todoroki Shrine and Todoroki 99 Falls. During the festival, men dressed in white robes carry a portable shrine ({{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}}) and rush down 140 steep stone steps from Todoroki Shrine, creating a dramatic and sacred spectacle{{Cite web|url=https://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |title=Todoroki Autumn Festival |publisher=Kaiyō Tourism Association |accessdate=2021-04-23 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130423021314/http://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp:80/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |archive-date=2013-04-23}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミヅハノメ}} (Goddess of Water) * {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto}} (deity of the Land) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命}} (Mountain God) == Transportation == * Take the {{ill|JR Shikoku|ja|四国旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線}} to {{ill|Awa-Kainan Station|ja|阿波海南駅}}, then transfer to a town bus to the "Todoroki Shrine" stop. The shrine is a 10-minute walk from the stop. == References == == External Links == * [https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html Todoroki Shrine] - Awa Navi {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kaiyō]] [[Category:History of Kaiyō]] [[ja:%E8%BD%9F%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E6%B5%B7%E9%99%BD%E7%94%BA)]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] [[Category:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]][[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]][[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] [[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] [[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] [[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] [[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]][[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

{{Shrine | name = Todoroki Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Kaifu District, Tokushima}} | location = {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Kaifu District, Tokushima|ja|海部郡 (徳島県)}} {{ill|Kaiyō|ja|海陽町}} Hirai Ōyo-godani 21-1 | coordinates = {{Coord|33|34|54.25|N|134|15|16.00|E|scale:5000_region:JP|display=inline}} | Deities = {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミヅハノメ}}
{{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi}} | Rank = | consecration_year = During the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era}} (1573–1593) | MainBuilding = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Ceremonies = }} '''Todoroki Shrine''' (轟神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kaiyō|ja|海陽町}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, Japan. == History == Todoroki Shrine was established during the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era}} (1573–1593). The surrounding {{ill|Todoroki 99 Falls|ja|轟九十九滝}} have been revered as a sacred site where divine spirits dwell. According to tradition, {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政}}, the founder of the {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩}}, prayed at the shrine for safe sea voyages during the {{ill|Imjin War|lt=Bunroku and Keichō Campaigns|ja|文禄・慶長の役}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html |title=Todoroki Shrine |publisher=Awa Navi |accessdate=2021-04-23}}. Each autumn, a grand festival is held at Todoroki Shrine and Todoroki 99 Falls. During the festival, men dressed in white robes carry a portable shrine ({{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}}) and rush down 140 steep stone steps from Todoroki Shrine, creating a dramatic and sacred spectacle{{Cite web|url=https://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |title=Todoroki Autumn Festival |publisher=Kaiyō Tourism Association |accessdate=2021-04-23 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130423021314/http://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp:80/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |archive-date=2013-04-23}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミヅハノメ}} (Goddess of Water) * {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto}} (deity of the Land) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi}} (Mountain God) == Transportation == * Take the {{ill|JR Shikoku|ja|四国旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線}} to {{ill|Awa-Kainan Station|ja|阿波海南駅}}, then transfer to a town bus to the "Todoroki Shrine" stop. The shrine is a 10-minute walk from the stop. == References == == External Links == * [https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html Todoroki Shrine] - Awa Navi {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kaiyō]] [[Category:History of Kaiyō]] [[ja:%E8%BD%9F%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E6%B5%B7%E9%99%BD%E7%94%BA)]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] [[Category:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]][[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]][[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]][[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] [[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] [[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] [[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] [[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]][[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:500 shortest pages as of 03-29-2025]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]][[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]][[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]][[Category:qq]]

The surrounding {{ill|Todoroki 99 Falls|ja|轟九十九滝}} have been revered as a sacred site where divine spirits dwell. The shrine is a 10-minute walk from the stop. == References == == External Links == * [https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html Todoroki Shrine] - Awa Navi {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kaiyō]] [[Category:History of Kaiyō]] [[ja:%E8%BD%9F%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E6%B5%B7%E9%99%BD%E7%94%BA)]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] [[Category:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]][[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]][[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]][[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] [[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] [[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] [[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] [[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]][[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:500 shortest pages as of 03-29-2025]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]][[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]][[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

During the festival, men dressed in white robes carry a portable shrine ({{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿|12=draft}}) and rush down 140 steep stone steps from Todoroki Shrine, creating a dramatic and sacred spectacle{{Cite web|url=https://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |title=Todoroki Autumn Festival |publisher=Kaiyō Tourism Association |accessdate=2021-04-23 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130423021314/http://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp:80/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |archive-date=2013-04-23}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミヅハノメ|12=draft}} (Goddess of Water) * {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} (deity of the Land) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=draft}} (Mountain God) == Transportation == * Take the {{ill|JR Shikoku|ja|四国旅客鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線|12=draft}} to {{ill|Awa-Kainan Station|ja|阿波海南駅|12=draft}}, then transfer to a town bus to the "Todoroki Shrine" stop. The shrine is a 10-minute walk from the stop. == References == == External Links == * [https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html Todoroki Shrine] - Awa Navi {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kaiyō]] [[Category:History of Kaiyō]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:500 shortest pages as of 03-29-2025]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:%E8%BD%9F%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E6%B5%B7%E9%99%BD%E7%94%BA)]] [[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]]

{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=draft|12=draft}} | Rank = | consecration_year = During the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|12=draft|12=draft}} (1573–1593) | MainBuilding = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Ceremonies = }} '''Todoroki Shrine''' (轟神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kaiyō|ja|海陽町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. == History == Todoroki Shrine was established during the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|12=draft|12=draft}} (1573–1593). The surrounding {{ill|Todoroki 99 Falls|ja|轟九十九滝|12=draft|12=draft}} have been revered as a sacred site where divine spirits dwell. According to tradition, {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政|12=draft|12=draft}}, the founder of the {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩|12=draft|12=draft}}, prayed at the shrine for safe sea voyages during the {{ill|Imjin War|lt=Bunroku and Keichō Campaigns|ja|文禄・慶長の役|12=draft|12=draft}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html |title=Todoroki Shrine |publisher=Awa Navi |accessdate=2021-04-23}}. Each autumn, a grand festival is held at Todoroki Shrine and Todoroki 99 Falls. During the festival, men dressed in white robes carry a portable shrine ({{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿|12=draft|12=draft}}) and rush down 140 steep stone steps from Todoroki Shrine, creating a dramatic and sacred spectacle{{Cite web|url=https://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |title=Todoroki Autumn Festival |publisher=Kaiyō Tourism Association |accessdate=2021-04-23 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130423021314/http://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp:80/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |archive-date=2013-04-23}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミヅハノメ|12=draft|12=draft}} (Goddess of Water) * {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}} (deity of the Land) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=draft|12=draft}} (Mountain God) == Transportation == * Take the {{ill|JR Shikoku|ja|四国旅客鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線|12=draft|12=draft}} to {{ill|Awa-Kainan Station|ja|阿波海南駅|12=draft|12=draft}}, then transfer to a town bus to the "Todoroki Shrine" stop. The shrine is a 10-minute walk from the stop. == References == == External Links == * [https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html Todoroki Shrine] - Awa Navi {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kaiyō]] [[Category:History of Kaiyō]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]] }} [[ja:%E8%BD%9F%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E6%B5%B7%E9%99%BD%E7%94%BA)]] [[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]]

revision.comment: 12 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:轟神社 (海陽町) up to rev 99755166
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 轟神社 |画像= {{画像募集中|cat=徳島県海部郡}} |所在地= [[徳島県]][[海部郡 (徳島県)|海部郡]][[海陽町]]平井字王余魚谷21-1 |位置= |祭神= [[ミヅハノメ|水象女命]]
[[国狭槌尊]]
[[大山祇命]] |社格= |創建= [[天正]]年間（[[1573年]]-[[1593年]]） |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= ||緯度度=33|緯度分=34|緯度秒=54.25|経度度=134|経度分=15|経度秒=16.00}} '''轟神社'''（とどろきじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[海陽町]]にある[[神社]]である。 == 歴史 == [[天正]]年間（[[1573年]]-[[1593年]]）に社殿を建立。周囲にある[[轟九十九滝]]を神霊の坐すところとして現在に至るまで信仰されている。また[[徳島藩]]の藩祖である[[蜂須賀家政]]が、[[文禄・慶長の役|朝鮮出兵]]にあたり海上安全を祈願したとも伝えられている{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html|title=轟神社|publisher=阿波ナビ|accessdate=2021-04-23}}。 毎年秋には轟神社および轟九十九滝で例大祭が行われ、白装束に身を包んだ男達が轟神社から御輿を担ぎ140段の急な石段を駆け下りる{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki|title=轟秋祭り|publisher=海陽町観光協会|accessdate=2021-04-23|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130423021314/http://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp:80/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki|archive-date=2013-04-23}}。 == 祭神 == * [[ミヅハノメ|水象女命]] * [[国狭槌尊]] * [[大山祇命]] == 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[牟岐線]]「[[阿波海南駅]]」下車より町営バス「轟神社」下車、徒歩で約10分。 == 脚注 == == 外部リンク == * [https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html 轟神社] - 阿波ナビ {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ととろきしんしや}} [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:海陽町の建築物]] [[Category:海陽町の歴史]]

{{神社 |名称= 轟神社 |画像= {{画像募集中|cat=徳島県海部郡}} |所在地= [[徳島県]][[海部郡 (徳島県)|海部郡]][[海陽町]]平井字王余魚谷21-1 |位置= |祭神= [[ミヅハノメ|水象女命]]
[[国狭槌尊]]
[[大山祇命]] |社格= |創建= [[天正]]年間（[[1573年]]-[[1593年]]） |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= ||緯度度=33|緯度分=34|緯度秒=54.25|経度度=134|経度分=15|経度秒=16.00}} '''轟神社'''（とどろきじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[海陽町]]にある[[神社]]である。 == 歴史 == [[天正]]年間（[[1573年]]-[[1593年]]）に社殿を建立。周囲にある[[轟九十九滝]]を神霊の坐すところとして現在に至るまで信仰されている。また[[徳島藩]]の藩祖である[[蜂須賀家政]]が、[[文禄・慶長の役|朝鮮出兵]]にあたり海上安全を祈願したとも伝えられている{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html|title=轟神社|publisher=阿波ナビ|accessdate=2021-04-23}}。 毎年秋には轟神社および轟九十九滝で例大祭が行われ、白装束に身を包んだ男達が轟神社から御輿を担ぎ140段の急な石段を駆け下りる{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki|title=轟秋祭り|publisher=海陽町観光協会|accessdate=2021-04-23|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130423021314/http://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp:80/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki|archive-date=2013-04-23}}。 == 祭神 == * [[ミヅハノメ|水象女命]] * [[国狭槌尊]] * [[大山祇命]] == 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[牟岐線]]「[[阿波海南駅]]」下車より町営バス「轟神社」下車、徒歩で約10分。 == 脚注 == == 外部リンク == * [https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html 轟神社] - 阿波ナビ {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ととろきしんしや}} [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:海陽町の建築物]] [[Category:海陽町の歴史]]

{{Shrine | name = Todoroki Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Kaifu District, Tokushima}} | location = {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kaifu District, Tokushima|ja|海部郡 (徳島県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kaiyō|ja|海陽町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Hirai Ōyo-godani 21-1 | coordinates = {{Coord|33|34|54.25|N|134|15|16.00|E|scale:5000_region:JP|display=inline}} | Deities = {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミヅハノメ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Rank = | consecration_year = During the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1573–1593) | MainBuilding = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Ceremonies = }} '''Todoroki Shrine''' (轟神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kaiyō|ja|海陽町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. == History == Todoroki Shrine was established during the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1573–1593). The surrounding {{ill|Todoroki 99 Falls|ja|轟九十九滝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} have been revered as a sacred site where divine spirits dwell. According to tradition, {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the founder of the {{ill|Tokushima Domain|ja|徳島藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, prayed at the shrine for safe sea voyages during the {{ill|Imjin War|lt=Bunroku and Keichō Campaigns|ja|文禄・慶長の役|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html |title=Todoroki Shrine |publisher=Awa Navi |accessdate=2021-04-23}}. Each autumn, a grand festival is held at Todoroki Shrine and Todoroki 99 Falls. During the festival, men dressed in white robes carry a portable shrine ({{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and rush down 140 steep stone steps from Todoroki Shrine, creating a dramatic and sacred spectacle{{Cite web|url=https://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |title=Todoroki Autumn Festival |publisher=Kaiyō Tourism Association |accessdate=2021-04-23 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130423021314/http://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp:80/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |archive-date=2013-04-23}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミヅハノメ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Goddess of Water) * {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (deity of the Land) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Mountain God) == Transportation == * Take the {{ill|JR Shikoku|ja|四国旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to {{ill|Awa-Kainan Station|ja|阿波海南駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, then transfer to a town bus to the "Todoroki Shrine" stop. The shrine is a 10-minute walk from the stop. == References == == External Links == * [https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html Todoroki Shrine] - Awa Navi {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|轟神社 (海陽町)|version=99755166|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kaiyō]] [[Category:History of Kaiyō]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]] }} [[ja:%E8%BD%9F%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E6%B5%B7%E9%99%BD%E7%94%BA)]] [[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]]

{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} | Rank = | consecration_year = During the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1593) | MainBuilding = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Ceremonies = }} '''Todoroki Shrine''' (轟神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kaiyō, Tokushima|Kaiyō]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. == History == Todoroki Shrine was established during the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1593). The surrounding {{ill|Todoroki 99 Falls|ja|轟九十九滝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Todoroki 99 Falls|qq=}} have been revered as a sacred site where divine spirits dwell. According to tradition, [[:en:Hachisuka Iemasa|Hachisuka Iemasa]], the founder of the [[:en:Tokushima Domain|Tokushima Domain]], prayed at the shrine for safe sea voyages during the [[:en:Imjin War|Bunroku and Keichō Campaigns]]{{Cite web|url=https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html |title=Todoroki Shrine |publisher=Awa Navi |accessdate=2021-04-23}}. Each autumn, a grand festival is held at Todoroki Shrine and Todoroki 99 Falls. During the festival, men dressed in white robes carry a portable shrine ([[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]) and rush down 140 steep stone steps from Todoroki Shrine, creating a dramatic and sacred spectacle{{Cite web|url=https://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |title=Todoroki Autumn Festival |publisher=Kaiyō Tourism Association |accessdate=2021-04-23 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130423021314/http://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp:80/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |archive-date=2013-04-23}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[:en:Mizuhanome|Mizuhanome]] (Goddess of Water) * {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (deity of the Land) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (Mountain God) == Transportation == * Take the [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR Shikoku]] [[:en:Mugi Line|Mugi Line]] to [[:en:Awa-Kainan Station|Awa-Kainan Station]], then transfer to a town bus to the "Todoroki Shrine" stop. The shrine is a 10-minute walk from the stop. == References == == External Links == * [https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html Todoroki Shrine] - Awa Navi {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|轟神社 (海陽町)|version=99755166|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kaiyō]] [[Category:History of Kaiyō]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:%E8%BD%9F%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E6%B5%B7%E9%99%BD%E7%94%BA)]] [[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] [[Category:徳島県海部郡の画像提供依頼]] [[Category:海陽町の建築物]] [[Category:海陽町の歴史]] [[ja:轟神社 (海陽町)]]

During the festival, men dressed in white robes carry a portable shrine ([[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]) and rush down 140 steep stone steps from Todoroki Shrine, creating a dramatic and sacred spectacle{{Cite web|url=https://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |title=Todoroki Autumn Festival |publisher=Kaiyō Tourism Association |accessdate=2021-04-23 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130423021314/http://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp:80/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |archive-date=2013-04-23}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[:en:Mizuhanome|Mizuhanome]] (Goddess of Water) * {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (deity of the Land) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (Mountain God) == Transportation == * Take the [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR Shikoku]] [[:en:Mugi Line|Mugi Line]] to [[:en:Awa-Kainan Station|Awa-Kainan Station]], then transfer to a town bus to the "Todoroki Shrine" stop. The shrine is a 10-minute walk from the stop. == References == == External Links == * [https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html Todoroki Shrine] - Awa Navi {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|轟神社 (海陽町)|version=99755166|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:%E8%BD%9F%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E6%B5%B7%E9%99%BD%E7%94%BA)]] [[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] [[Category:徳島県海部郡の Image Requests]] [[Category:海陽町の建築物]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kaiyo]] [[ja:轟神社 (海陽町)]]

{{Shrine | name = Todoroki Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Kaifu District, Tokushima}} | location = [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Kaifu District, Tokushima|Kaifu District, Tokushima]] [[:en:Kaiyō, Tokushima|Kaiyō]] Hirai Ōyo-godani 21-1 | coordinates = {{Coord|33|34|54.25|N|134|15|16.00|E|scale:5000_region:JP|display=inline}} | Deities = [[:en:Mizuhanome|Mizuhanome]]
{{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} | Rank = | consecration_year = During the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1593) | MainBuilding = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Ceremonies = }} '''Todoroki Shrine''' (轟神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kaiyō, Tokushima|Kaiyō]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. == History == Todoroki Shrine was established during the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1593). The surrounding {{ill|Todoroki 99 Falls|ja|轟九十九滝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Todoroki 99 Falls|qq=}} have been revered as a sacred site where divine spirits dwell. According to tradition, [[:en:Hachisuka Iemasa|Hachisuka Iemasa]], the founder of the [[:en:Tokushima Domain|Tokushima Domain]], prayed at the shrine for safe sea voyages during the [[:en:Imjin War|Bunroku and Keichō Campaigns]]{{Cite web|url=https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html |title=Todoroki Shrine |publisher=Awa Navi |accessdate=2021-04-23}}. Each autumn, a grand festival is held at Todoroki Shrine and Todoroki 99 Falls. During the festival, men dressed in white robes carry a portable shrine ([[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]) and rush down 140 steep stone steps from Todoroki Shrine, creating a dramatic and sacred spectacle{{Cite web|url=https://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |title=Todoroki Autumn Festival |publisher=Kaiyō Tourism Association |accessdate=2021-04-23 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130423021314/http://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp:80/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |archive-date=2013-04-23}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[:en:Mizuhanome|Mizuhanome]] (Goddess of Water) * {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (deity of the Land) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (Mountain God) == Transportation == * Take the [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR Shikoku]] [[:en:Mugi Line|Mugi Line]] to [[:en:Awa-Kainan Station|Awa-Kainan Station]], then transfer to a town bus to the "Todoroki Shrine" stop. The shrine is a 10-minute walk from the stop. == References == == External Links == * [https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html Todoroki Shrine] - Awa Navi {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|轟神社 (海陽町)|version=99755166|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:%E8%BD%9F%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E6%B5%B7%E9%99%BD%E7%94%BA)]] [[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] [[Category:Image requests in Kaifu District, Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:海陽町の建築物]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kaiyo]] [[ja:轟神社 (海陽町)]]

The shrine is a 10-minute walk from the stop. == References == == External Links == * [https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html Todoroki Shrine] - Awa Navi {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|轟神社 (海陽町)|version=99755166|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:E8%BD%9F%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E6%B5%B7%E9%99%BD%E7%94%BA)]] [[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] [[Category:Image requests in Kaifu District, Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kaiyo Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kaiyo]] [[ja:轟神社 (海陽町)]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]]

{{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} | Rank = | consecration_year = During the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1593) | MainBuilding = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Ceremonies = }} '''Todoroki Shrine''' (轟神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kaiyō, Tokushima|Kaiyō]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. == History == Todoroki Shrine was established during the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1593). The surrounding {{ill|Todoroki 99 Falls|ja|轟九十九滝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Todoroki 99 Falls|qq=}} have been revered as a sacred site where divine spirits dwell. According to tradition, [[:en:Hachisuka Iemasa|Hachisuka Iemasa]], the founder of the [[:en:Tokushima Domain|Tokushima Domain]], prayed at the shrine for safe sea voyages during the [[:en:Imjin War|Bunroku and Keichō Campaigns]]{{Cite web|url=https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html |title=Todoroki Shrine |publisher=Awa Navi |accessdate=2021-04-23}}. Each autumn, a grand festival is held at Todoroki Shrine and Todoroki 99 Falls. During the festival, men dressed in white robes carry a portable shrine ([[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]) and rush down 140 steep stone steps from Todoroki Shrine, creating a dramatic and sacred spectacle{{Cite web|url=https://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |title=Todoroki Autumn Festival |publisher=Kaiyō Tourism Association |accessdate=2021-04-23 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130423021314/http://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp:80/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |archive-date=2013-04-23}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[:en:Mizuhanome|Mizuhanome]] (Goddess of Water) * {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (deity of the Land) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (Mountain God) == Transportation == * Take the [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR Shikoku]] [[:en:Mugi Line|Mugi Line]] to [[:en:Awa-Kainan Station|Awa-Kainan Station]], then transfer to a town bus to the "Todoroki Shrine" stop. The shrine is a 10-minute walk from the stop. == References == == External Links == * [https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html Todoroki Shrine] - Awa Navi {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|轟神社 (海陽町)|version=99755166|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:E8%BD%9F%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E6%B5%B7%E9%99%BD%E7%94%BA)]] [[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] [[Category:Image requests in Kaifu District, Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kaiyo Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kaiyo]] [[ja:轟神社 (海陽町)]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]]

During the festival, men dressed in white robes carry a portable shrine ([[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]) and rush down 140 steep stone steps from Todoroki Shrine, creating a dramatic and sacred spectacle{{Cite web|url=https://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |title=Todoroki Autumn Festival |publisher=Kaiyō Tourism Association |accessdate=2021-04-23 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130423021314/http://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp:80/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |archive-date=2013-04-23}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[:en:Mizuhanome|Mizuhanome]] (Goddess of Water) * {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (deity of the Land) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (Mountain God) == Transportation == * Take the [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR Shikoku]] [[:en:Mugi Line|Mugi Line]] to [[:en:Awa-Kainan Station|Awa-Kainan Station]], then transfer to a town bus to the "Todoroki Shrine" stop. The shrine is a 10-minute walk from the stop. == References == == External Links == * [https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html Todoroki Shrine] - Awa Navi {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|轟神社 (海陽町)|version=99755166|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:E8%BD%9F%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E6%B5%B7%E9%99%BD%E7%94%BA)]] [[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] [[Category:Image requests in Kaifu District, Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kaiyo Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kaiyo]] [[ja:轟神社 (海陽町)]]

According to tradition, [[:en:Hachisuka Iemasa|Hachisuka Iemasa]], the founder of the [[:en:Tokushima Domain|Tokushima Domain]], prayed at the shrine for safe sea voyages during the [[:en:Imjin War|Bunroku and Keichō Campaigns]]{{Cite web|url=https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html |title=Todoroki Shrine |publisher=Awa Navi |accessdate=2021-04-23}}. Each autumn, a grand festival is held at Todoroki Shrine and Todoroki 99 Falls. During the festival, men dressed in white robes carry a portable shrine ([[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]) and rush down 140 steep stone steps from Todoroki Shrine, creating a dramatic and sacred spectacle{{Cite web|url=https://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |title=Todoroki Autumn Festival |publisher=Kaiyō Tourism Association |accessdate=2021-04-23 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130423021314/http://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp:80/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |archive-date=2013-04-23}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[:en:Mizuhanome|Mizuhanome]] (Goddess of Water) * {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (deity of the Land) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (Mountain God) == Transportation == * Take the [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR Shikoku]] [[:en:Mugi Line|Mugi Line]] to [[:en:Awa-Kainan Station|Awa-Kainan Station]], then transfer to a town bus to the "Todoroki Shrine" stop. The shrine is a 10-minute walk from the stop. == References == == External Links == * [https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html Todoroki Shrine] - Awa Navi {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|轟神社 (海陽町)|version=99755166|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:E8%BD%9F%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E6%B5%B7%E9%99%BD%E7%94%BA)]] [[sn:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] [[Category:Image requests in Kaifu District, Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kaiyo Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kaiyo]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:轟神社 (海陽町)]]

{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} | Rank = | consecration_year = During the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1593) | MainBuilding = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Ceremonies = | location = {{ill|Kaiyo|qid=Q1205954}} }} '''Todoroki Shrine''' (轟神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kaiyō, Tokushima|Kaiyō]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. == History == Todoroki Shrine was established during the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1593). The surrounding {{ill|Todoroki 99 Falls|ja|轟九十九滝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Todoroki 99 Falls|qq=|1=Todoroki 99 Waterfalls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637784}} have been revered as a sacred site where divine spirits dwell. According to tradition, [[:en:Hachisuka Iemasa|Hachisuka Iemasa]], the founder of the [[:en:Tokushima Domain|Tokushima Domain]], prayed at the shrine for safe sea voyages during the [[:en:Imjin War|Bunroku and Keichō Campaigns]]{{Cite web|url=https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html |title=Todoroki Shrine |publisher=Awa Navi |accessdate=2021-04-23}}. Each autumn, a grand festival is held at Todoroki Shrine and Todoroki 99 Falls. During the festival, men dressed in white robes carry a portable shrine ([[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]) and rush down 140 steep stone steps from Todoroki Shrine, creating a dramatic and sacred spectacle{{Cite web|url=https://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |title=Todoroki Autumn Festival |publisher=Kaiyō Tourism Association |accessdate=2021-04-23 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130423021314/http://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp:80/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |archive-date=2013-04-23}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[:en:Mizuhanome|Mizuhanome]] (Goddess of Water) * {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (deity of the Land) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (Mountain God) == Transportation == * Take the [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR Shikoku]] [[:en:Mugi Line|Mugi Line]] to [[:en:Awa-Kainan Station|Awa-Kainan Station]], then transfer to a town bus to the "Todoroki Shrine" stop. The shrine is a 10-minute walk from the stop. == References == == External Links == * [https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html Todoroki Shrine] - Awa Navi {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|轟神社 (海陽町)|version=99755166|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[Category:Image requests in Kaifu District, Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kaiyo Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kaiyo]] [[ja:轟神社 (海陽町)]] {{wikidata link|Q106852497}}

{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} | Rank = | consecration_year = During the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1593) | MainBuilding = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Ceremonies = | location = {{ill|Kaiyo|WD=Q1205954}} }} '''Todoroki Shrine''' (轟神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kaiyō, Tokushima|Kaiyō]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. == History == Todoroki Shrine was established during the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1593). The surrounding {{ill|Todoroki 99 Falls|ja|轟九十九滝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Todoroki 99 Falls|1=Todoroki 99 Waterfalls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637784}} have been revered as a sacred site where divine spirits dwell. According to tradition, [[:en:Hachisuka Iemasa|Hachisuka Iemasa]], the founder of the [[:en:Tokushima Domain|Tokushima Domain]], prayed at the shrine for safe sea voyages during the [[:en:Imjin War|Bunroku and Keichō Campaigns]]{{Cite web|url=https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html |title=Todoroki Shrine |publisher=Awa Navi |accessdate=2021-04-23}}. Each autumn, a grand festival is held at Todoroki Shrine and Todoroki 99 Falls. During the festival, men dressed in white robes carry a portable shrine ([[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]) and rush down 140 steep stone steps from Todoroki Shrine, creating a dramatic and sacred spectacle{{Cite web|url=https://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |title=Todoroki Autumn Festival |publisher=Kaiyō Tourism Association |accessdate=2021-04-23 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130423021314/http://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp:80/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |archive-date=2013-04-23}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[:en:Mizuhanome|Mizuhanome]] (Goddess of Water) * {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (deity of the Land) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (Mountain God) == Transportation == * Take the [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR Shikoku]] [[:en:Mugi Line|Mugi Line]] to [[:en:Awa-Kainan Station|Awa-Kainan Station]], then transfer to a town bus to the "Todoroki Shrine" stop. The shrine is a 10-minute walk from the stop. == References == == External Links == * [https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html Todoroki Shrine] - Awa Navi {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|轟神社 (海陽町)|version=99755166|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[Category:Image requests in Kaifu District, Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kaiyo Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kaiyo]] [[ja:轟神社 (海陽町)]] {{wikidata link|Q106852497}}

{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} | Rank = | consecration_year = During the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1593) | MainBuilding = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Ceremonies = | location = {{ill|Kaiyo|WD=Q1205954}} }} '''Todoroki Shrine''' (轟神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kaiyō, Tokushima|Kaiyō]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. == History == Todoroki Shrine was established during the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1593). The surrounding {{ill|Todoroki 99 Falls|ja|轟九十九滝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Todoroki 99 Falls|1=Todoroki 99 Waterfalls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637784}} have been revered as a sacred site where divine spirits dwell. According to tradition, [[:en:Hachisuka Iemasa|Hachisuka Iemasa]], the founder of the [[:en:Tokushima Domain|Tokushima Domain]], prayed at the shrine for safe sea voyages during the [[:en:Imjin War|Bunroku and Keichō Campaigns]]{{Cite web|url=https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html |title=Todoroki Shrine |publisher=Awa Navi |accessdate=2021-04-23}}. Each autumn, a grand festival is held at Todoroki Shrine and Todoroki 99 Falls. During the festival, men dressed in white robes carry a portable shrine ([[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]) and rush down 140 steep stone steps from Todoroki Shrine, creating a dramatic and sacred spectacle{{Cite web|url=https://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |title=Todoroki Autumn Festival |publisher=Kaiyō Tourism Association |accessdate=2021-04-23 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130423021314/http://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp:80/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |archive-date=2013-04-23}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[:en:Mizuhanome|Mizuhanome]] (Goddess of Water) * {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (deity of the Land) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (Mountain God) == Transportation == * Take the [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR Shikoku]] [[:en:Mugi Line|Mugi Line]] to [[:en:Awa-Kainan Station|Awa-Kainan Station]], then transfer to a town bus to the "Todoroki Shrine" stop. The shrine is a 10-minute walk from the stop. == References == == External Links == * [https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html Todoroki Shrine] - Awa Navi {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|轟神社 (海陽町)|version=99755166|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[Category:Image requests in Kaifu District, Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kaiyo Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kaiyo]] [[ja:轟神社 (海陽町)]] {{wikidata link|Q106852497}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q106852497}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

The surrounding {{ill|Todoroki 99 Falls|ja|轟九十九滝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Todoroki 99 Falls|1=Todoroki 99 Waterfalls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637784}} have been revered as a sacred site where divine spirits dwell. The shrine is a 10-minute walk from the stop. == References == == External Links == * [https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html Todoroki Shrine] - Awa Navi {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|轟神社 (海陽町)|version=99755166|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[Category:Image requests in Kaifu District, Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kaiyo Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kaiyo]] [[ja:轟神社 (海陽町)]] {{wikidata link|Q106852497}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q106852497}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

The shrine is a 10-minute walk from the stop. == References == == External Links == * [https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html Todoroki Shrine] - Awa Navi {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|轟神社 (海陽町)|version=99755166|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[Category:Image requests in Kaifu District, Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kaiyo Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kaiyo]] [[ja:轟神社 (海陽町)]] {{wikidata link|Q106852497}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q106852497}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} | Rank = | consecration_year = During the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1593) | MainBuilding = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Ceremonies = | location = {{ill|Kaiyo|WD=Q1205954}} }} '''Todoroki Shrine''' (轟神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kaiyō, Tokushima|Kaiyō]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. == History == Todoroki Shrine was established during the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1593). The surrounding {{ill|Todoroki 99 Falls|ja|轟九十九滝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Todoroki 99 Falls|1=Todoroki 99 Waterfalls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637784}} have been revered as a sacred site where divine spirits dwell. According to tradition, [[:en:Hachisuka Iemasa|Hachisuka Iemasa]], the founder of the [[:en:Tokushima Domain|Tokushima Domain]], prayed at the shrine for safe sea voyages during the [[:en:Imjin War|Bunroku and Keichō Campaigns]]{{Cite web|url=https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html |title=Todoroki Shrine |publisher=Awa Navi |accessdate=2021-04-23}}. Each autumn, a grand festival is held at Todoroki Shrine and Todoroki 99 Falls. During the festival, men dressed in white robes carry a portable shrine ([[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]) and rush down 140 steep stone steps from Todoroki Shrine, creating a dramatic and sacred spectacle{{Cite web|url=https://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |title=Todoroki Autumn Festival |publisher=Kaiyō Tourism Association |accessdate=2021-04-23 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130423021314/http://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp:80/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |archive-date=2013-04-23}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[:en:Mizuhanome|Mizuhanome]] (Goddess of Water) * {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (deity of the Land) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (Mountain God) == Transportation == * Take the [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR Shikoku]] [[:en:Mugi Line|Mugi Line]] to [[:en:Awa-Kainan Station|Awa-Kainan Station]], then transfer to a town bus to the "Todoroki Shrine" stop. The shrine is a 10-minute walk from the stop. == References == == External Links == * [https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html Todoroki Shrine] - Awa Navi {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|轟神社 (海陽町)|version=99755166|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[Category:Image requests in Kaifu District, Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kaiyo Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kaiyo]] [[ja:轟神社 (海陽町)]] {{wikidata link|Q106852497}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q106852497}} [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

During the festival, men dressed in white robes carry a portable shrine ([[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]) and rush down 140 steep stone steps from Todoroki Shrine, creating a dramatic and sacred spectacle{{Cite web|url=https://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |title=Todoroki Autumn Festival |publisher=Kaiyō Tourism Association |accessdate=2021-04-23 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130423021314/http://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp:80/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |archive-date=2013-04-23}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[:en:Mizuhanome|Mizuhanome]] (Goddess of Water) * {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (deity of the Land) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (Mountain God) == Transportation == * Take the [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR Shikoku]] [[:en:Mugi Line|Mugi Line]] to [[:en:Awa-Kainan Station|Awa-Kainan Station]], then transfer to a town bus to the "Todoroki Shrine" stop. The shrine is a 10-minute walk from the stop. == References == == External Links == * [https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html Todoroki Shrine] - Awa Navi {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|轟神社 (海陽町)|version=99755166|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[Category:Image requests in Kaifu District, Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kaiyo Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kaiyo]] [[ja:轟神社 (海陽町)]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q106852497}}

During the festival, men dressed in white robes carry a portable shrine ([[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]) and rush down 140 steep stone steps from Todoroki Shrine, creating a dramatic and sacred spectacle{{Cite web|url=https://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |title=Todoroki Autumn Festival |publisher=Kaiyō Tourism Association |accessdate=2021-04-23 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130423021314/http://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp:80/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |archive-date=2013-04-23}}. == Enshrined Deities == * [[:en:Mizuhanome|Mizuhanome]] (Goddess of Water) * {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (deity of the Land) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (Mountain God) == Transportation == * Take the [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR Shikoku]] [[:en:Mugi Line|Mugi Line]] to [[:en:Awa-Kainan Station|Awa-Kainan Station]], then transfer to a town bus to the "Todoroki Shrine" stop. The shrine is a 10-minute walk from the stop. == References == == External Links == * [https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html Todoroki Shrine] - Awa Navi {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|轟神社 (海陽町)|version=99755166|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:轟神社 (海陽町)]] {{wikidata link|Q106852497}} [[Category:Buildings in Kaiyo Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Image requests in Kaifu District, Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kaiyo]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} | Rank = | consecration_year = During the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} (1573–1593) | MainBuilding = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = | Ceremonies = | location = {{ill|Kaiyo|WD=Q1205954}} }} '''Todoroki Shrine''' (轟神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kaiyō, Tokushima|en|Kaiyō, Tokushima|WD=Q1205954|lt=Kaiyō}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|WD=Q160734|lt=Tokushima Prefecture}}, Japan. == History == Todoroki Shrine was established during the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} (1573–1593). The surrounding {{ill|Todoroki 99 Falls|ja|轟九十九滝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Todoroki 99 Falls|1=Todoroki 99 Waterfalls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637784}} have been revered as a sacred site where divine spirits dwell. According to tradition, {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|en|Hachisuka Iemasa|WD=Q1073557|lt=Hachisuka Iemasa}}, the founder of the {{ill|Tokushima Domain|en|Tokushima Domain|WD=Q3034557|lt=Tokushima Domain}}, prayed at the shrine for safe sea voyages during the {{ill|Imjin War|en|Imjin War|WD=Q576338|lt=Bunroku and Keichō Campaigns}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html |title=Todoroki Shrine |publisher=Awa Navi |accessdate=2021-04-23}}. Each autumn, a grand festival is held at Todoroki Shrine and Todoroki 99 Falls. During the festival, men dressed in white robes carry a portable shrine ({{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=Mikoshi}}) and rush down 140 steep stone steps from Todoroki Shrine, creating a dramatic and sacred spectacle{{Cite web|url=https://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |title=Todoroki Autumn Festival |publisher=Kaiyō Tourism Association |accessdate=2021-04-23 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130423021314/http://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp:80/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |archive-date=2013-04-23}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Mizuhanome|en|Mizuhanome|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhanome}} (Goddess of Water) * {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (deity of the Land) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (Mountain God) == Transportation == * Take the {{ill|Shikoku Railway Company|en|Shikoku Railway Company|WD=Q496531|lt=JR Shikoku}} {{ill|Mugi Line|en|Mugi Line|WD=Q860478|lt=Mugi Line}} to {{ill|Awa-Kainan Station|en|Awa-Kainan Station|WD=Q4829614|lt=Awa-Kainan Station}}, then transfer to a town bus to the "Todoroki Shrine" stop. The shrine is a 10-minute walk from the stop. == References == == External Links == * [https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html Todoroki Shrine] - Awa Navi {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|轟神社 (海陽町)|version=99755166|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:轟神社 (海陽町)]] {{wikidata link|Q106852497}} [[Category:Buildings in Kaiyo Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Image requests in Kaifu District, Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kaiyo]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

During the festival, men dressed in white robes carry a portable shrine ({{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=Mikoshi}}) and rush down 140 steep stone steps from Todoroki Shrine, creating a dramatic and sacred spectacle{{Cite web|url=https://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |title=Todoroki Autumn Festival |publisher=Kaiyō Tourism Association |accessdate=2021-04-23 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130423021314/http://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp:80/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |archive-date=2013-04-23}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Mizuhanome|en|Mizuhanome|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhanome}} (Goddess of Water) * {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (deity of the Land) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (Mountain God) == Transportation == * Take the {{ill|Shikoku Railway Company|en|Shikoku Railway Company|WD=Q496531|lt=JR Shikoku}} {{ill|Mugi Line|en|Mugi Line|WD=Q860478|lt=Mugi Line}} to {{ill|Awa-Kainan Station|en|Awa-Kainan Station|WD=Q4829614|lt=Awa-Kainan Station}}, then transfer to a town bus to the "Todoroki Shrine" stop. The shrine is a 10-minute walk from the stop. == References == == External Links == * [https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html Todoroki Shrine] - Awa Navi {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|轟神社 (海陽町)|version=99755166|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:轟神社 (海陽町)]] {{wikidata link|Q106852497}} [[Category:Buildings in Kaiyo Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Image requests in Kaifu District, Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kaiyo]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

According to tradition, {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|en|Hachisuka Iemasa|WD=Q1073557|lt=Hachisuka Iemasa|1=Hachisuka Iemasa}}, the founder of the {{ill|Tokushima Domain|en|Tokushima Domain|WD=Q3034557|lt=Tokushima Domain|1=Tokushima Domain}}, prayed at the shrine for safe sea voyages during the {{ill|Imjin War|en|Imjin War|WD=Q576338|lt=Bunroku and Keichō Campaigns|1=Imjin War}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html |title=Todoroki Shrine |publisher=Awa Navi |accessdate=2021-04-23}}. Each autumn, a grand festival is held at Todoroki Shrine and Todoroki 99 Falls. During the festival, men dressed in white robes carry a portable shrine ({{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=Mikoshi|1=Mikoshi}}) and rush down 140 steep stone steps from Todoroki Shrine, creating a dramatic and sacred spectacle{{Cite web|url=https://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |title=Todoroki Autumn Festival |publisher=Kaiyō Tourism Association |accessdate=2021-04-23 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130423021314/http://www.kaiyo-kankou.jp:80/index.php/event2/todorokifes-aki |archive-date=2013-04-23}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Mizuhanome|en|Mizuhanome|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhanome|1=Mizuhanome}} (Goddess of Water) * {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (deity of the Land) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (Mountain God) == Transportation == * Take the {{ill|Shikoku Railway Company|en|Shikoku Railway Company|WD=Q496531|lt=JR Shikoku|1=Shikoku Railway Company}} {{ill|Mugi Line|en|Mugi Line|WD=Q860478|lt=Mugi Line|1=Mugi Line}} to {{ill|Awa-Kainan Station|en|Awa-Kainan Station|WD=Q4829614|lt=Awa-Kainan Station|1=Awa-Kainan Station}}, then transfer to a town bus to the "Todoroki Shrine" stop. The shrine is a 10-minute walk from the stop. == References == == External Links == * [https://www.awanavi.jp/spot/20495.html Todoroki Shrine] - Awa Navi {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|轟神社 (海陽町)|version=99755166|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:轟神社 (海陽町)]] {{wikidata link|Q106852497}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kaiyo Town]] [[Category:Image requests in Kaifu District, Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kaiyo]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
